Fixed the interval merge bug in [src/interval_tree.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_001_amp-deep3_5exoonbk/src/interval_tree.py). The root cause was that overlap checks were using strict `<` comparisons, which treated closed intervals like `[1, 3]` and `[3, 5]` as non-overlapping. I changed the overlap logic to use inclusive comparisons and made `merge_overlapping()` reuse that same overlap/merge behavior so touching intervals now collapse correctly.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed`, and a direct check of merging `[1, 3]` with `[3, 5]` returns `[Interval(1, 5)]`.

Fixed the LRU eviction bug in [src/lru_cache.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_002_amp-deep3_3wmksjk9/src/lru_cache.py#L72-L85) by changing the full-cache path in `put()` to evict the oldest `OrderedDict` entry with `popitem(last=False)`. The previous code was removing the newest entry and also tried to `move_to_end()` a key before insertion.

Verification passed with `python -m pytest -q` and the suite is now green: `6 passed`. I also checked the eviction flow directly and confirmed that after inserting `a`, `b`, then `c` into a capacity-2 cache, only `a` is evicted.

Fixed the overflow in [`TokenBucket.refill()`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_003_amp-deep3_qkm26e4n/src/rate_limiter.py#L55-L64) by clamping the post-refill token count to `self._capacity` instead of letting elapsed time accumulate unbounded tokens.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q tests\test_rate_limiter.py` now passes with `6 passed`, including the capacity-cap regression cases.

Fixed the off-by-one bug in [src/spiral.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_006_amp-deep3_zvwdfx5n/src/spiral.py#L47-L51) by changing the left-boundary update from `left += 2` to `left += 1`. Adjusted the rotated-array search boundary check in [src/rotated_search.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_007_amp-deep3_4yzk_xnn/src/rotated_search.py#L34-L39) so the left sorted half includes `nums[left]` when deciding whether to search that side. The bug was the strict comparison `nums[left] < target`, which skipped valid matches at the left boundary like `4` in `[4,5,6,7,0,1,2]` and `1` in `[1,2,3,4,5,6,7]`.

Verified with `python -m pytest -q`: `7 passed`. I also spot-checked the reported cases, and they now return `0`, `0`, and `4` as expected.

It now expands every parsed pair.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `6 passed`.

Fixed the escaped-pipe parsing bug in [`src/markdown_table.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_009_amp-deep3_8c2hm871/src/markdown_table.py). `_split_row()` now scans each row character-by-character, treats `\|` as a literal pipe inside the current cell, and still uses unescaped `|` as the column delimiter. That fixes escaped pipes in both headers and data rows without changing the separator-row logic or normal table behavior.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `6 passed`.

Fixed the cron day-of-week bug in [src/cron_parser.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_010_amp-deep3_qxoci14c/src/cron_parser.py#L123-L141) by correcting the Python-to-cron weekday conversion in `_matches()`. It now maps Python’s `weekday()` output to standard cron numbering (`0=Sunday, 1=Monday, ..., 6=Saturday`) instead of the old Monday-first mapping.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes, with all 6 tests green.

Fixed the queue logic in [src/async_queue.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_1ge7vp2b/src/async_queue.py#L47-L95) so `put()` now truly applies async backpressure by awaiting `asyncio.Queue.put()` instead of dropping items when full. I also moved `get_count` accounting into [`get()`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_1ge7vp2b/src/async_queue.py#L60-L75) itself and removed the duplicate increment from [`consumer()`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_1ge7vp2b/src/async_queue.py#L120-L130).

There was one follow-on deadlock in [`drain()`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_1ge7vp2b/src/async_queue.py#L81-L95): it emptied the queue synchronously and returned before blocked producers had a chance to resume. It now yields once when the queue becomes empty so pending `put()` calls can complete and be drained as well. Verified with `python -m pytest -q`: `7 passed`.

Fixed the stale memoization bug in [src/memoize.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_012_amp-deep3_9zp6fnwe/src/memoize.py). The cache key builder no longer uses object identity via `id(...)`; it now recursively freezes arguments into stable hashable representations, with type tags for containers so equal lists/dicts hit the same cache entry and mutating a list after a call produces a different key on the next call.

Verification passed with `python -m pytest -q` and `python -m compileall src tests`. All tests are now green: `6 passed`.

Updated [`src/observer.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_013_amp-deep3_i6kl04c6/src/observer.py#L42-L60) so `EventEmitter.emit()` iterates over a snapshot of the listeners present at the start of the emit cycle instead of the live list.

That fixes the mutation-during-iteration bug:
- listeners that remove themselves or others no longer cause later listeners to be skipped
- `once()` listeners no longer skip subsequent listeners
- listeners added during `emit()` still do not run until the next emit

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `6 passed`.

Implemented the fix in [src/di_container.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_014_amp-deep3_fiks_j1v/src/di_container.py#L37-L96). The container now keeps an active resolution stack, detects when a service is requested twice in the same chain, and raises `CircularDependencyError` with a cycle message like `A -> B -> A` instead of recursing until `RecursionError` ([cycle check](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_014_amp-deep3_fiks_j1v/src/di_container.py#L74-L79), [stack push/pop](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_014_amp-deep3_fiks_j1v/src/di_container.py#L87-L91)).

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `6 passed`.

Fixed the shortest-path bug in [src/dijkstra.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_015_amp-deep3_zyvr59kl/src/dijkstra.py). The algorithm now treats `0` as a valid edge weight instead of skipping it, ignores stale heap entries before processing a node, and only returns the destination after that entry is confirmed current. I also added a small cycle guard in `all_shortest_paths()` so zero-cost cycles don’t recurse forever when shortest paths are enumerated.

Verification passed with `python -m pytest -q` (`7 passed`), and I also checked a zero-weight cycle case with `python -c`, which returned the expected distances and path.

Fixed the FSM bug in [src/fsm.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_016_amp-deep3_ab7pnun_/src/fsm.py#L62-L83) by removing the fallback that accepted an event from the wrong source state. `trigger()` now raises `InvalidTransitionError` immediately when `(current_state, event)` is not registered, before any callbacks, history updates, or state mutation occur.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `7 passed`.

Implemented the precedence fix in [src/evaluator.py](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_017_amp-deep3_66jofq80/src/evaluator.py#L80-L146). The parser now evaluates `*` and `/` in a dedicated `parse_term()` step before applying `+` and `-` in `parse_expr()`, so expressions like `3 + 4 * 2` now produce `11` as expected. I also added a final EOF check so trailing unexpected tokens are rejected instead of being silently ignored.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes, with all 7 tests green.

Fixed the connection pool bug in [src/conn_pool.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_018_amp-deep3_qrevu1ki/src/conn_pool.py#L92-L123) so closed connections are never handed back out. `acquire()` now drains and discards unhealthy pooled connections before returning one, and `release()` now skips re-adding a connection if its health check fails.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes with `6 passed`.

That gives every call its own fresh retry budget and fixes the “immediately fail on later calls” behavior. I applied the same correction to `retry_async`, since it had the identical bug.

I also added one focused regression test in [tests/test_retry.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_019_amp-deep3_1rtowtm7/tests/test_retry.py#L1-L139) to cover the async path, which previously had no protection against this exact issue.

Updated [src/topo_sort.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_020_amp-deep3_ly58lj14/src/topo_sort.py) so the DFS topo sort now tracks an in-progress `visiting` set. That lets it detect back-edges and self-loops correctly, raising `CyclicGraphError` instead of silently returning an invalid order. Valid DAG behavior is unchanged: nodes are still added only after all dependencies are processed.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passed with `7 passed`, and a direct runtime check confirmed both a valid DAG ordering and cycle detection.

Fixed the delete rebalancing bug in [`src/rbtree.py`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_021_amp-deep3_u5l0mtv2/src/rbtree.py#L185-L215). In both `_delete_fixup` branches, when the sibling’s children are both black, the code now correctly promotes `x` to its parent instead of stepping to `x.parent.left` or `x.parent.right`. That preserves the standard red-black “double black bubbles upward” behavior and prevents nodes from becoming unreachable after later rotations.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` now passes with `8 passed`, and the previously failing deletion sequence keeps the tree valid with the expected in-order traversal.

That restores the correct topological order, so dependency roots load before the plugins that require them.

Verified with `python -m pytest -q` and the suite now passes: `7 passed`. I also confirmed the previously broken chain now resolves as `['database', 'orm', 'api']`, and `load_all()` initializes in dependency-safe order.

Updated [src/type_validator.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_025_amp-deep3_07rx2jhe/src/type_validator.py) so container validation is subtype-aware instead of doing exact inner-type matches. Lists, dicts, and sets now validate their contents by delegating back through `validate`, which means values like `Dog` are accepted where `Animal` is expected, including inside containers.

I also fixed schema-to-schema compatibility so type parameters are checked recursively and covariantly. That makes schemas like `List[Dog]` compatible with `List[Animal]` and `Dict[str, Dog]` compatible with `Dict[str, Animal]`.

Verification: `python -m pytest tests\\test_module.py -q` passed with `6 passed`.

That makes the entire `get()` read-and-reorder path atomic, so concurrent readers can’t reorder or access the `OrderedDict` after the lock has been released.

The transformer now detects whether a nested function or lambda introduces its own binding for the old name and skips renaming within that inner scope, while still renaming true outer-scope references.

Verification passed with `python -m pytest -q` and `python -m compileall src`.

Fixed the error-handler ordering bug in [src/middleware.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_028_amp-deep3_lou6zxsc/src/middleware.py#L62-L66) by changing `handle_error()` to append handlers instead of prepending them. That preserves FIFO registration order, so outer handlers run first, `get_error_handler_names()` returns the expected order, and the first handler can transform an error before later fallback handlers run.

Verified with `python -m pytest tests\test_module.py -q`: `6 passed`.

**The fix:** In `redistribute_from_right` (line 192), changed `parent->keys[idx] = borrowed` to `parent->keys[idx] = right->keys[0]`.

**Why it was broken:** When borrowing the first key from the right sibling, the parent separator was set to the borrowed key's value. But that key now lives in the left node, not the right. The B+ tree invariant requires the separator to equal the first key of the right child. With the wrong separator, `upper_bound` would route searches for the borrowed key to the right child where it no longer exists, causing `search()` to return false and range queries to miss elements.
